key_insights.insight: White noise machines can mask external noises and improve sleep quality, especially adaptive ones that adjust to sudden loud sounds.
key_insights.quote: So if the door slams, then the volume temporarily increasesand then will decrease.

key_insights.insight: A cooler room temperature, ideally between 60-67 degrees Fahrenheit, promotes better sleep.
key_insights.quote: 60 to 67 degrees has been shown to help individuals stay asleep and fall asleep.

key_insights.insight: Taking a hot shower or bath close to bedtime can disrupt sleep because it raises core body temperature, which needs to drop for sleep initiation.
key_insights.quote: When you are taking a hot bath or hot shower,it increases your core temperature.But when you fall asleep, your core temperature drops.

key_insights.insight: Shifting your shower or bath to an hour and a half before bed can help lower your core temperature and enhance deep sleep.
key_insights.quote: If you back that shower up an hour and a half, that should be sufficient time thatwe think potentially there's an augmentation of helping that drop in temperature.

key_insights.insight: Having a small, balanced snack of complex carbohydrates and lean protein before bed can prevent hunger-related sleep disturbances.
key_insights.quote: I'm okay with you having a pre-sleep snack...that means we'll go for something like 50%of a complex carb and like 50% of a lean protein.

key_insights.insight: Cereal and milk can be an easy, accessible presleep snack.
key_insights.quote: Cereal and milk is an easy one that many people have access to.

actionable_advice.how_to: Reduce the temperature by 1-2 degrees every few days until you find the optimal temperature for sleep.

actionable_advice.advice: Shift hot showers or baths to earlier in the evening.
actionable_advice.how_to: Take your shower at least 1.5 hours before your bedtime to allow your body temperature to drop.

actionable_advice.advice: Prepare a pre-sleep snack if you get hungry before bed.
actionable_advice.how_to: Combine a complex carbohydrate (like whole grain cereal or crackers) with a lean protein (like milk, cottage cheese, or peanut butter).

contrarian_claims: It's okay to listen to something relaxing while falling asleep if you are accustomed to it.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Moment 185- The One Thing You Should Never Do Before Bed: Dr Cheri Mah
